description: Create strategy for No Shared Fate. Covers: factory-based test setup (each test gets fresh scoped state), per-test isolation for parallel safety, no shared package-level mutable state in tests, tests that work in imperfect conditions (not requiring pristine DB). Also covers: defaults that match production — test helpers should default to production-realistic configuration, not convenient fictions (e.g., in-memory store when prod uses PostgreSQL). Divergent defaults are a FEAL concern that manifests in test setup. Go guidelines to define underneath: per-test factory pattern, t.Parallel() usage, scoped test identities (unique user/team/document per test), no package-level var in test files holding mutable state. Cross-references: related to 'Compliance Test Suites' (uses factory pattern).
title: Create strategy: Isolated Test Execution

close_reason: Wrote docs/WORKFLOW.md covering all 8 required topics; merged strategy-creation-workflow.md content into it
description: Document the bjornstack workflow for human consumption. Must cover: (1) The four layers with WHY/HOW/WHAT framing. (2) File organization: principles/<name>.md, implementation/strategies/<name>.md, implementation/<lang>/<name>/guidelines.md, implementation/<lang>/<name>/divergences.md. (3) How the skills drive the pipeline. (4) The audit command — what it checks (missing strategies, missing guidelines, stale guidelines, undocumented divergences), how to run it. (5) The canonical list of supported languages. (6) How to add a new supported language. (7) Guard candidate identification and tracking. (8) Judgment-only and not-applicable handling.
title: Write docs/WORKFLOW.md

description: Create strategy for Be Your Name and Compute or Coordinate. Covers: testing through public interface rather than internals, preventing coupling to implementation details, test helpers that call t.Helper() so failures point at the test case. Go guidelines to define underneath: _test package convention (package foo_test), t.Helper() in all functions that call require.*/assert.*, exception for internal/ packages. Guard candidates: lint for missing _test package suffix, lint for test helpers missing t.Helper(). Cross-references: related to 'Coordinator Test Design'.
title: Create strategy: Public API Surface Testing

description: Create strategy for Structure Is Intent. Covers: organizing code by domain (cart/, pricing/, notifications/) not by technical role (models/, controllers/, services/), package boundaries that constrain where code belongs, eliminating catch-all packages. Go guidelines to define underneath: package naming conventions, no utils/helpers/common packages, internal/ usage patterns. Guard candidates: lint for banned package names (utils, helpers, common, misc).
title: Create strategy: Domain-Driven Package Structure

description: Create strategy for Earned Abstraction. Covers: the rule of three (don't abstract until 3 concrete cases), recognizing the boolean-mode smell as a signal of premature abstraction, refactoring mode-flag abstractions into strategy pattern (named collaborator + shared core/harness), when duplication is preferable to abstraction. Go guidelines to define underneath: function types as strategy collaborators, extracting shared core with varying strategy, recognizing boolean/mode parameters as a smell. Guard candidates: lint for boolean parameters that switch behavior in otherwise shared functions. Cross-references: upstream of 'Coordinator Test Design' (CoC), connects to Consistent Beats Correct (keep duplication over wrong abstraction).
title: Create strategy: Earned Abstraction

description: Create strategy for Be Your Name. Covers: BDD-style naming where test names describe situation and expected outcome, names that read as sentences and serve as living specifications, long names preferred over vague short names. Go guidelines to define underneath: Test<Type>_<Method> outer function naming, subtest/table case names as BDD scenarios, 'expected X to Y' assertion message phrasing. Cross-references: complements 'Parameterized Test Structure'.
title: Create strategy: Scenario Naming

description: Create strategy for Highlight the Difference and Fail Early and Loud. Covers: table-driven test layout for 3+ similar cases, strategy function pattern (replace if/else with function-typed struct fields), all fields explicit in every test case, uniform test body executing same code path for every case. Go guidelines to define underneath: table test struct conventions, strategy function signatures, no optional struct fields, individual tests for 1-2 cases. Guard candidates: lint for if/switch in test body functions. Cross-references: related to 'Test Data Builders' and 'Whole-Object Assertion'.
title: Create strategy: Parameterized Test Structure

description: Create strategy for Fail Early and Loud and Highlight the Difference. Covers: comparing complete objects instead of cherry-picking fields, injecting deterministic sources for timestamps/UUIDs, using builders for expected values. Go guidelines to define underneath: require.* over assert.*, non-deterministic field handling patterns (inject clocks, UUID generators, or zero-and-compare). Guard candidates: lint for >N field-by-field assertions when a builder exists for the type. Cross-references: tightly related to strategies 'Test Data Builders' and 'Parameterized Test Structure'.
title: Create strategy: Whole-Object Assertion

close_reason: Guard-designer skill created with REJECTS/ACCEPTS format, 5 modes, 6 quality checks, 9 edge cases. WORKFLOW.md updated.
description: Create a new skill at .claude/skills/guard-designer/SKILL.md. The skill handles the fourth layer (CHECK) of the four-layer system. It takes a guard candidate from guidelines-editor (a hypothesis that something can be mechanically checked) and designs the guard: what tool (linter rule, AST check, CI gate), what it checks, false positive assessment, nolint escape hatch design, and implementation plan. May produce an epic + subtasks for complex guards. Triggered when someone picks up a 'Design guard' bead created by guidelines-editor.
title: Create guard-designer skill

close_reason: Implemented four-layer system: created strategies/FORMAT.md and strategies/DIVERGENCE.md, updated README.md, principles/FORMAT.md, and principle-editor SKILL.md
description: Define what a strategy is within bjornstack. This is the foundational task — all other workflow decisions depend on it. Must produce: (1) Document how the four layers relate through the WHY/HOW/WHAT frame: Principle=WHY (why it matters), Strategy=HOW (the abstract approach), Guideline=WHAT (concrete language-specific steps), Guard=enforcement of WHAT. This framing should be part of README.md and any format docs. (2) A format spec for strategy files (like FORMAT.md for principles). What fields? What level of detail? What makes a good vs bad strategy? (3) Clear definition of the boundary between strategy and guideline — when does something belong in the strategy vs in a language-specific guideline? (4) When is a strategy needed vs when a principle is direct enough to skip straight to guidelines? (5) Define the divergence documentation format — how to record when a language can't follow part of a strategy. (6) Update README.md to reflect the four-layer system with WHY/HOW/WHAT framing. (7) Create a FORMAT equivalent for strategies.
title: Define strategy as a concept

title: Define strategy creation workflow

description: Define and document the end-to-end bjornstack workflow for the four-layer system (principle → strategy → guideline → guard). Uses the file system as source of truth and an audit command for completeness checks. Expected output: docs/WORKFLOW.md. This epic is blocked by all 7 component tasks.
title: Define bjornstack workflow

close_reason: Added After approval section with uniqueness check, file writing, and strategy handoff steps
description:
Add an 'After approval' section to .claude/skills/principle-editor/SKILL.md. Steps after user approves: (1) Uniqueness check — glob principles/*.md (excluding FORMAT.md), compare names and definitions for collision or semantic overlap. (2) Write principle to principles/<kebab-name>.md. (3) Check if the principle would benefit from a strategy (signal: 'so what do I do instead?' or layer check caught a strategy disguised as principle). If yes, offer to create a beads task for the strategy-editor. Never force. Not every principle needs a strategy.

NOTE: The handoff to strategy-editor section has already been added to the principle-editor skill. This issue covers the remaining 'After approval' workflow (uniqueness check, file writing).

Reference: docs/strategy-creation-workflow.md for full workflow context.
title: Enhance principle-editor skill

close_reason: All 7 gaps addressed: annotated example, wait-for-approval, Update mode, Review output structure, Extract bead routing, raw input shaping, density guidance
description:
Building the strategy-editor surfaced several patterns the principle-editor predates and doesn't have. Review and apply:

Structural gaps:
1. No annotated example — add one showing why each part of a real principle works
2. No explicit 'wait for approval' step in Create mode — agent could write to disk without pausing
3. No Update mode — principles evolve, need a mode for revising existing ones
4. No Review mode output structure — should lead with summary, then failures with rewrites
5. Extract mode doesn't create beads for other layers — should route cross-layer handoffs through beads

Consistency gaps:
6. No shaping step for raw input — users arrive with vague rules or frustrations, need help forming a one-sentence principle before drafting
7. No density guidance — when to use 1 vs 2 violations, how long WHY should be

Reference: .claude/skills/strategy-editor/SKILL.md for the patterns to mirror.
title: Review principle-editor against strategy-editor patterns

close_reason: Created guidelines-editor skill with full SKILL.md, references symlinks to strategies/FORMAT.md and strategies/DIVERGENCE.md
description: Create a new skill at .claude/skills/guidelines-editor/SKILL.md. The skill: (1) Reads the source principle and its strategy. Refuses to proceed if no strategy exists — prompts user to create one first. (2) Targets a specific language. (3) Walks through the strategy point by point, deriving concrete language-specific guidelines. (4) For each strategy point, checks: can this be followed as-is in this language? If not, documents the divergence and why in implementation/<lang>/<name>/divergences.md. (5) For each guideline, assesses guard candidacy ('Can this be mechanically checked with zero judgment?'). (6) Writes guidelines to implementation/<lang>/<name>/guidelines.md, including a 'Guard candidates' section. (7) Creates a bead for each guard candidate. (8) For judgment-only or not-applicable principles, writes a file explaining why.
title: Create guidelines-editor skill

close_reason: Strategy-editor skill created at .claude/skills/strategy-editor/SKILL.md with all 20 improvements from iterative review
description:
Create a new skill at .claude/skills/strategy-editor/SKILL.md. Three modes (mirroring principle-editor): Create, Review, Extract.

Create mode: (1) User describes a technique or provides a principle name. (2) Draft the strategy in strategies/FORMAT.md format. (3) Run quality checks: name is 2-4 word noun phrase, PRINCIPLE link references a real principle, steps are numbered/abstract/language-agnostic, OUTCOMES are observable, MISAPPLICATION is realistic, SKIP WHEN is present. (4) Layer check: not a principle (needs multiple steps), not a guideline (no language references), not a tutorial. (5) Coherence check: steps follow from principle, outcomes satisfy the principle, misapplication is realistic, skip-when is honest. (6) Write to strategies/<kebab-name>.md. (7) Offer handoff to guidelines-editor.

Review mode: Evaluate a draft strategy against all quality checks. Show concrete rewrites for failures.

Extract mode: Given prose docs or best practices, identify strategies vs. other layers. Produce formatted strategies.

Reference: docs/strategy-creation-workflow.md for full workflow context.
title: Create strategy-editor skill

Go guidelines to define underneath: a.* builder conventions in test/a/ package, options pattern for SUT, Build() returns value not pointer, static UUIDs for reproducibility. Cross-references: related to 'Whole-Object Assertion' and 'Parameterized Test Structure'.
title: Create strategy: Test Data Builders

title: Build principle audit script

description: Create strategy for No Shared Fate and Structure Is Intent. Covers: services communicate through APIs or messages, not shared databases. Each service owns its data and exposes it through explicit interfaces. No reaching into another service's tables. Architecture-level — guidelines are per-stack. Hard line: if two services need the same data, one owns it and the other requests it.
title: Create strategy: Service Boundary Communication
